message:
test(golden): factor capture_robot_json helper and add models status golden

Golden harness was already hardcoded to one command (`cass capabilities
--json`) and the capture/scrub/assert logic was inlined in that one
test. Adding a second golden (`cass models status --json`) would have
copy-pasted the wrapper, so factor it first.

tests/golden_robot_json.rs:
- Extract `capture_robot_json(test_home, args)` that runs `cass <args>`
  in the isolated TempDir, asserts a zero exit status, and returns
  `String` stdout. On non-zero exit, the panic message now includes
  BOTH stdout and stderr — previously only stderr was surfaced, which
  hid the common case where cass printed the real error body on stdout.
- Use `unwrap_or_else(|err| panic!(...))` for the spawn step so the
  panic message names the exact argv and underlying `io::Error` instead
  of a generic "run cass capabilities --json" string. Helps when
  diagnosing which command hung in CI.
- Rewrite `capabilities_json_matches_golden` to call the new helper.
- Add `models_status_json_matches_golden`: runs `cass models status
  --json` and asserts the scrubbed output against the new
  `tests/golden/robot/models_status.json.golden`.

tests/golden/robot/models_status.json.golden:
- Frozen scrubbed output for the "no model acquired" state. Captures
  the full contract surface: `model_id`, `state`, `state_detail`,
  `next_step`, `lexical_fail_open`, `revision`, `license`,
  `total_size_bytes`, `observed_file_bytes`, `policy_source`,
  `cache_lifecycle` nested block, and recorded diagnostics. Dynamic
  paths get scrubbed to `[TEST_HOME]/...` by the existing scrubber so
  the golden is reproducible across machines.

Regenerate with:
    UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --test golden_robot_json -- models_status

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
